title: Baby-safe bottom balm
subtitle: A gentle anhydrous barrier balm with zinc oxide and calendula-infused oil for nappy rash
excerpt: This balm contains no essential oils, no fragrance, and no water. It relies on zinc oxide to form a physical barrier between the skin and moisture, and on calendula-infused oil for its well-documented soothing properties. The formula is deliberately simple.
sourceNotes: Zinc oxide barrier cream formulation based on standard pharmaceutical and natural cosmetics references. Calendula oil properties from Journal of Wound Care.

recipe.yieldDescription: 60 g bottom balm (fills a 60 ml tin)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Make multiple tins at once.
recipe.temperatureNote: Melt shea and coconut oil gently. Add calendula oil and zinc oxide once cooled to below 40 degrees Celsius.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a cool place. Discard if the balm develops an off smell or unusual texture.

glossaryTerms.definition: A mineral powder that forms a physical barrier on the skin's surface, repelling moisture and protecting against irritation. Non-nano zinc oxide particles are too large to be absorbed through the skin and are the appropriate grade for baby products and sunscreen.

A carrier oil in which dried calendula (marigold) flowers have been steeped, transferring the plant's anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing compounds into the oil. Available ready-made or made at home by cold-infusing dried calendula in olive or sunflower oil for 4 to 6 weeks.
Containing no water. Anhydrous baby products are safe from microbial growth without a preservative and are suitable for broken or sensitive skin where a water-based product could carry bacteria.
A fat from the shea tree, rich in oleic and stearic acids. It is well-tolerated by most skin types, including sensitive baby skin, and is widely used as the base for balms and barrier creams.

content.text: providing the physical barrier. There are no essential oils in this formula: baby skin is sensitive and the scalp and genital area are not appropriate sites for concentrated aromatic compounds. Zinc oxide powder must be handled with a dust mask; it is safe once incorporated into the balm but should not be inhaled during weighing. Shelf life is 12 months.

items.prepNote: 10% of formula; wear a dust mask when weighing

content.text: Weigh the zinc oxide powder into a small bowl before you begin heating anything, wearing a dust mask. Set aside. Melt the shea butter and coconut oil together in a double boiler until just liquid. Remove from heat and add the calendula-infused oil. Allow the mixture to cool to below 40 degrees Celsius.

Add the zinc oxide powder to the cooled oil mixture and stir thoroughly until evenly distributed. There should be no visible white clumps. Pour into the tin and leave to set for 1 hour at room temperature.

Store in a cool, dry place. Shelf life is 12 months. The balm will be a pale yellow-white and is opaque from the zinc oxide; this is normal. Discard if the scent changes to rancid or the texture shows oil pooling.
